reasoning: The index has shown a strong secular uptrend since mid-2025, rising from ~61.7 to 70.5. However, there has been a sharp downward break in momentum in the final 3 days of February, falling from 70.54 to 70.28. Since this is a 7-day trailing average, this indicates extremely low daily values recently, which will continue to pull the average down over the next week. Therefore, the Mar 8 value is highly likely to be below 70.28. In previous post-COVID years (2024, 2025), March has typically been a down month relative to late February, despite Indeed's 2017-2019 seasonal adjustments. This adds to the likelihood of a weak Mar 8. For Mar 31, there is more uncertainty: the short-term negative momentum and 'March slump' pattern compete against the strong 9-month macro recovery trend (+1 pt/month). Consequently, the probability of exceeding 70.28 by the end of March is drawn closer to a coin flip (0.52).
